meta.name: Joseph Murphy
meta.title: Lead Software Engineer
meta.tagline: Software developer with a passion for web services and architecture.
meta.description: Lead Software Engineer at Assurant with a passion for web services and clean architecture. I drive technical leadership on large-scale insurance platforms, mentor engineers, and advocate for clean architecture and testing best practices.

bullets: Lead a team of 6 engineers on the API side of a large-scale insurance platform serving multinational enterprise clients, owning architectural direction, technical standards, and cross-functional alignment.

Architected and leading delivery of a public-facing integration API enabling DMS clients and OEM partners to embed quoting and policy booking directly into their platforms via REST — actively partnering with a DMS client and a major OEM toward a production launch.

Identified zero test coverage as a critical risk and initiated the project's entire test suite from scratch, personally driving it from 0% to 55% coverage and establishing testing as a first-class engineering practice.

Established team-wide PR standards including conventional commits, enforced PR title conventions, and GitHub Actions gates that block merges on failing tests — reducing QA-stage regressions by an estimated 30% and bringing consistency and accountability to every code review.
Led a platform-wide performance initiative, auditing and eliminating legacy data sources and bottlenecks that unlocked measurable throughput gains across core services.
Partnered with business and data stakeholders to rationalize and streamline API surface area, decommissioning irrelevant services and improving overall system cohesion.
Actively grow junior and mid-level engineers through structured code reviews, architectural mentorship, and a culture of engineering excellence.

Championed the adoption of AI-assisted development across the team, integrating GitHub Copilot into daily development workflows and the code review process to accelerate delivery and surface issues earlier in the feedback loop.
Led all technical decisions as co-founder, defining the full system architecture for an iOS application and its supporting Web API from concept through production.
Owned the end-to-end engineering roadmap, balancing product velocity with architectural integrity in an early-stage startup environment.
Drove the strategic migration from a monolithic architecture to a hybrid microservices model, improving scalability and reducing inter-system coupling.
Led full-stack development of an international insurance platform in Laravel and championed the adoption of Vue.js as the front-end standard across the engineering team.
Spearheaded a package-driven refactor of core business logic into reusable Laravel packages, enabling consistent delivery across a suite of applications.

highlights: Lambda Chi Alpha — Treasurer, Vice President

projects.name: Assurant Quoting API
projects.description: Global quoting API enabling international partners to generate insurance quotes and apply for coverage programmatically. Designed for scale across multinational clients.

projects.name: Follow App
projects.description: Co-founded and architected a full iPhone application and Web API from scratch. Owned all technical decisions from infrastructure to app store deployment.

projects.name: EPG International Insurance System
projects.description: Built an international insurance platform with Laravel and spearheaded the front-end migration to Vue.js. Refactored monolithic core into reusable PHP packages.

projects.name: Automated Test Suite
projects.description: Comprehensive test infrastructure at Assurant to enforce quality standards across teams, reduce regressions, and enable confident large-scale deployments.

projects.name: HandlersHub
projects.description: Full-stack SaaS platform for dog clubs and sporting event organizers. Features state machine-driven event lifecycle management, club membership with Stripe billing, breed and health record tracking, and AI-powered marketing content generation.

projects.name: Web Crawler
projects.description: Production-ready distributed web crawler with a Redis-backed priority queue, multi-worker crawl pipeline, sentiment analysis, and a FastAPI layer over PostgreSQL storage. Designed for scale with full Docker orchestration.
